dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 22, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Cardinals Fall to Marlins, Alvarez Powers Astros to Shutout**

In a one-sided affair, the Miami Marlins dominated the St. Louis Cardinals 4-1, with Iván Herrera's 3rd home run of the season not enough for the Cardinals. Herrera's fly ball to left center field was the lone highlight for the Cardinals. Meanwhile, the Houston Astros shut out the Cleveland Guardians 2-0 behind Peter Lambert's 8-strikeout performance.

Yordan Alvarez was the offensive star for the Astros, going 3-for-4 with a home run and 2 RBIs. His 11th home run of the season came on a fly ball to right center field, scoring Carlos Correa.

In other games, the Baltimore Orioles edged the Kansas City Royals 8-6, with Coby Mayo's home run and 3 RBIs leading the Orioles. The Los Angeles Angels defeated the Toronto Blue Jays 7-3, with Nolan Schanuel's 4-hit, 4-RBI performance powering the Angels.

The Seattle Mariners came from behind to defeat the Athletics 5-4, with Cal Raleigh's 3-hit, 1-home run performance helping the Mariners. The Detroit Tigers beat the Milwaukee Brewers 5-2, with Casey Mize's 7-strikeout performance leading the Tigers.

The Atlanta Braves outlasted the Washington Nationals 8-6, with Michael Harris II's 2-home run, 3-RBI performance leading the Braves. The New York Yankees shut out the Boston Red Sox 4-1 behind Max Fried's 9-strikeout performance.

The New York Mets edged the Minnesota Twins 3-2, with Connor Prielipp's 6-strikeout performance leading the Mets. The Chicago Cubs defeated the Philadelphia Phillies 7-2, with Seiya Suzuki's 2-hit, 2-RBI performance powering the Cubs.

The Pittsburgh Pirates outlasted the Texas Rangers 8-4, with Oneil Cruz's 7th home run of the season leading the Pirates. The Colorado Rockies crushed the San Diego Padres 8-3, with Tomoyuki Sugano's 5-strikeout performance leading the Rockies.

The Arizona Diamondbacks came from behind to defeat the Chicago White Sox 11-7, with Ildemaro Vargas' 2-home run, 5-RBI performance leading the Diamondbacks. The San Francisco Giants shut out the Los Angeles Dodgers 3-0 behind Shohei Ohtani's 7-strikeout performance.
